channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Trump Says Israel, Hezbollah to Halt Attacks as Iran Talks Continue
item.description: Tehran had said it would suspend negotiations over Israeli strikes in Lebanon.

item.title: A Trump Deal With Iran Could Spell Trouble for Netanyahu
item.description: The Israeli leader needs a win ahead of a key election.

item.title: Do you really want that computer-science degree?
item.description: American universities are selling ever more, just when their value is eroding

item.title: Can the stockmarket swallow Anthropic, SpaceX and OpenAI?
item.description: Watch out for indigestion

item.title: Pakistan’s Diplomatic Pivot Makes It a Trump-Era Power Player
item.description: Islamabad is happy to give the U.S. president the image he craves.

item.title: Abelardo de la Espriella is now the front-runner in Colombia
item.description: The right-wing populist outperformed in the first round of a deeply polarised election

item.title: Colombia’s populist, Bukele-loving right looks likely to win power
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella outperformed in the first round of a deeply polarised presidential election

item.title: China’s delivery drivers are its most obvious underclass
item.description: New rules aim to help but the economy will keep them down

item.title: Vietnam’s Quiet Strategy at the Shangri-La Dialogue
item.description: At the Shangri-La Dialogue, Hanoi has sought to shape the terms of the regional security debate.

item.title: The Epidemic of GPS Jamming
item.description: Across the world, signals crucial for safe air and sea travel are being disrupted.

item.title: Uzbekistan’s Emergence as Central Asia’s Mobility Hub
item.description: Behind Uzbekistan's record visitor numbers, rooted in social and economic ties, lies a larger story of regional reintegration expanding Tashkent's influence across Central Asia.

item.title: How to bring down cheap, low-flying drones
item.description: Acoustics and innovative radar could help

item.title: The One Man Who Could Give Trump Middle East Peace
item.description: With a large majority of UN member states recognizing Palestine on the 1967 lines, there is a clear international blueprint for creating an independent Palestinian state alongside Israel. What has been missing is credible Palestinian leadership of the kind that the imprisoned politician Marwan Barghouti could provide.

item.title: Ukraine is now Europe’s war. Survival can’t be the only aim
item.description: America’s disengagement means it is now the old continent’s conflict to manage

item.title: The Real Crisis in Andhra Pradesh is Development, Not Demography
item.description: When the Andhra government cannot sustain existing welfare systems, why is it promising financial incentives to couples to have three or four children?

item.title: Taiwan and Poland on the Frontline of Hybrid Conflict
item.description: An interview with Aleksandra Gasztold on what Taiwan and Eastern Europe can learn from each other.

item.title: Washington Wants Myanmar’s Minerals
item.description: Democracy promotion is out, and the race to engage the country’s junta is on.

item.title: Can the State Department Make Europe Great Again?
item.description: A small but influential office will soon announce grants to support Trump administration causes in Europe.

item.title: Europe’s Nuclear Options
item.description: The risks raised by nuclear weapons remain as potent as ever, but the institutional framework that mitigated them has been severely eroded. The task now is to prevent deterrence from giving way to proliferation—which, for Europe, means building conventional capabilities, keeping the US engaged, and upholding restraint.

item.title: The EU’s New Economic Security Tools and China’s Countermeasure Calculus
item.description: The central issue in China-EU economic relations is no longer only trade imbalance, but the growing political anxiety in Europe over the speed of China’s industrial upgrading.

item.title: Can Pakistan Win Bangladesh’s Trust Without Apologizing for the Genocide of 1971?
item.description: The current warmth in Bangladesh-Pakistan bilateral relations is less a product of mutual trust and more a function of geopolitical calculations.

item.title: Why China Needs High GDP Growth Rates to Avoid a Crisis
item.description: While Western nations might view a 2 percent or 4 percent growth rate as a spectacular success, China faces a shock even with a slight dip in its much higher figures.

item.title: The Upcoming C5+1 Dialogue on Critical Minerals
item.description: On the sidelines of the Astana Mining & Metallurgy Congress, the C5+1 aims to deepen cooperation on critical minerals.

item.title: The Awakening of Koizumi Shinjiro
item.description: How a defense portfolio transformed Japan’s most underestimated politician.

item.title: The Intelligence That AI Is Missing
item.description: For AI to simplify humans’ complex social and professional lives, it cannot just give tidy answers based on whatever the internet has to say about our values. It will need the capacity to account for our relational commitments, which are typically grounded in personal identity, experience, and culture.

item.title: The 3 Geopolitical Shocks That Boosted the Middle Corridor
item.description: Could one of the most iconic trade routes of all time be making a modern comeback?

item.title: Why Isn’t Europe Poorer Than the US?
item.description: Nobel laureate Paul Krugman has a point when he says that European living standards have largely kept pace with those in the US, despite flagging growth and innovation. But this will not remain true indefinitely: restoring Europe's ability to compete at the technological frontier remains essential to its future well-being.

item.title: The Energy Crisis Will Long Outlast the Iran War
item.description: The baked-in damage to oil and gas production will take months to undo.

item.title: The Real Choice Confronting Developing Countries
item.description: Lost in the debate about whether developing countries should pursue manufacturing- or services-led growth is the potential for new AI tools to make even informal workers far more productive. If governments can broaden access to such technologies, they no longer need to choose between strategies.

item.title: Germany Is Scrambling to Speed Up Its Rearmament
item.description: Berlin has already made a huge commitment to its military—but is facing new obstacles at home and abroad.

item.title: Why Indonesia is Rebuilding Commodity Oversight
item.description: President Prabowo’s new initiative reflects a broader effort to address fragmented oversight, reduce commodity leakage, and strengthen his country's role in global supply chains.

item.title: Philippines, Vietnam Upgrade Diplomatic Partnership Amid Maritime Tensions With China
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. stated that maintaining peace, stability, and the freedom of navigation in the South China Sea "remains non-negotiable" for both nations.

item.title: Clinton’s Lincoln Bedroom Scandal Looks Quaint Now
item.description: What a very 1990s scandal says about corruption during the second Trump administration.

item.title: Ukraine Turns the Tide
item.description: Why a cease-fire is now a real possibility.

item.title: The End of Foreign Aid Is Not the End of Development
item.description: How the world can do more with less.

item.title: How Migration Helps Authoritarians
item.description: The costs of democratic drain.

item.title: The End of Foreign Aid Is Not the End of Development
item.description: How the world can do more with less.

item.title: Ukraine Turns the Tide
item.description: Why a cease-fire is now a real possibility.

item.title: What to Expect from the Impeachment Trial of the Philippine Vice President Duterte
item.description: Various contending political blocs are looking to capitalize politically on Sara Duterte's trial as they look ahead to the presidential election of 2028.

item.title: India Has Signed BrahMos Missile Deal With Vietnam, Indian Minister Says
item.description: The country is set to become the third in Southeast Asia to purchase the powerful Indo-Russian missile system, after the Philippines and Indonesia.
